volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 3.5.576c - Section 4.4.667)

sections.label: Section 3.5.576c
sections.original: Mihi .
sections.translation: To me .

sections.label: Section 3.5.576d
sections.original: Satis tuto tamen .
sections.translation: In perfect safety , at all events .

sections.label: Section 3.5.577
sections.original: Edicit ne vir quisquam ad eam adeat ; et mihi ne abscedam imperat ,
sections.translation: She gave orders that no male was to come near her , and commanded me not to stir away from her ; that I was to remain alone with her in the inner apartments . [*] Looking bashfully on the ground , I nodded assent .

sections.label: Section 3.5.579b
sections.original: Miser !
sections.translation: Poor fellow !

sections.label: Section 3.5.579c
sections.original: Ego , inquit , ad coenam hine eo .
sections.translation: I am going out , said she , to dinner .

sections.label: Section 3.5.580
sections.original: Abducit secum ancillas : paucae quae circum illam essent manent
sections.translation: She took her maids with her ; a few novices of girls [*] remained , to be about her . These immediately made preparations for her to bathe . I urged them to make haste . While preparations were being made , the damsel sat in a room looking up at a certain painting , [*] in which was represented how Jove [*] is said once to have sent a golden shower into the bosom of Danaë .

sections.label: Section 3.5.585
sections.original: Egomet quoque id spectare coepi ; et , quia consimilem luserat
sections.translation: I myself began to look at it as well , and as he had in former times played the like game , I felt extremely delighted that a God should change himself into money , and slily come through the tiles of another person ’ s house , to deceive the fair one by means of a shower . But what God was this ? He who shakes the most lofty temples of heaven with his thunders .

sections.label: Section 3.5.590
sections.original: Ego homuncio hoc non facerem ? ego illud vero ita feci ac lubens .
sections.translation: Was I , a poor creature of a mortal , [*] not to do the same ? Certainly , I was to do it , and without hesitation . While I was thinking over these matters with myself , the damsel meantime was fetched away to bathe ; she went , bathed , and came back ; after which they laid her on a couch . I stood waiting to see if they gave me any orders . One came up , Here , Dorus , said she , take this fan , [*] and let her have a little air in this fashion , while we are bathing ;

sections.label: Section 3.5.595
sections.original: Ubi nos laverimus si voles lavato . Accipio tristis .
sections.translation: when we have bathed , if you like , you may bathe too . With a demure air I took it .

sections.label: Section 3.5.596
sections.original: Tum equidem istuc os tuum impudens videre nimium vellem ;
sections.translation: Really , I should very much have liked to see that impudent face of yours just then , and what figure a great donkey like you made , holding a fan !

sections.label: Section 3.5.598
sections.original: Vix elocuta est hoc , foras simul omnes proruunt se :
sections.translation: Hardly had she said this , when all , in a moment , betook themselves off : away they went to bathe , and chattered aloud ; [*] just as the way is when masters are absent .

sections.label: Section 3.5.600
sections.original: Interea somnus virginem opprimit ; ego limis specto
sections.translation: Meanwhile , sleep overtook the damsel ; I slily looked askance [*] through the fan ; [*] this way ( showing how ) : at the same time I looked round in all directions , to see whether all was quite safe . I saw that it was . I bolted the door .

sections.label: Section 3.5.603
sections.original: Quid tum ?
sections.translation: What then ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.603b
sections.original: Quid ? quid tum ? fatue .
sections.translation: Eh ? What then , you simpleton ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.603c
sections.original: Fateor .
sections.translation: I own I am .

sections.label: Section 3.5.603d
sections.original: An ego occasionem
sections.translation: Was I to let slip the opportunity offered me , so excellent , so short-lived , [*] so longed for , so unexpected .

sections.label: Section 3.5.605
sections.original: Amitterem ? tum pol ego is essem vere qui simulabar .
sections.translation: In that case , i ’ faith , I really should have been the person I was pretending to be .

sections.label: Section 3.5.606
sections.original: Sane hercle ut dicis : sed interim de symbolis quid actum est ?
sections.translation: Troth , you certainly are in the right ; but , meantime , what has been arranged about the club-entertainment ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.607
sections.original: Paratum est .
sections.translation: All ’ s ready .

sections.label: Section 3.5.607b
sections.original: Frugi es . Ubi ? domino ?
sections.translation: You are a clever hand ; but where ? At your house ?

sections.label: Section 3.5.607c
sections.original: Immo apud libortum Discumn .
sections.translation: No , at Discus ’ s , our freedman .

sections.label: Section 3.5.608
sections.original: Perlonge est : sed tanto ocius properenus ; muta vestem .
sections.translation: That ’ s a long way off .

sections.label: Section 3.5.609
sections.original: Ubi mutem ? perii ; nam domo exulo nunc : metuo fratrem
sections.translation: Where am I to change it ? I ’ m at a loss ; for at present I ’ m an exile from home ; I ’ m afraid of my brother ,

sections.label: Section 3.5.610
sections.original: Ne intus sit ; porro autem pater ne rure redierit iam .
sections.translation: lest he should be in-doors : and then again of my father , lest he should have returned from the country by this .

sections.label: Section 3.5.611
sections.original: Eamus ad me : ibi proximum est ubi mutes .
sections.translation: Let ’ s go to my house ; there is the nearest place for you to change .

sections.label: Section 3.5.611b
sections.original: Recte dicis
sections.translation: You say right . Let ’ s be off ; besides , I want to take counsel with you about this girl , by what means I may be able to secure the future possession of her .

sections.label: Section 3.5.613b
sections.original: Fiat .
sections.translation: Very well .

sections.label: Section 4.1.614
sections.original: Ita me Di ament , quantum ego illum vidi , nonnihil timeo misera
sections.translation: So may the Gods bless me , but from what I have seen , I ’ m terribly afraid that

sections.label: Section 4.1.615
sections.original: Ne quam ille hodie insanus turbam faciat , aut vim Thaidi .
sections.translation: this mad fellow will be guilty of some disturbance to-day or of some violence to Thais . For when this young man , the brother of the damsel , arrived , she begged the Captain to order him to be admitted ; he immediately began to get into a passion , and yet didn ’ t dare refuse ; Thais still insisted that he would invite the man in . This she did for the sake of detaining him ; because there was no opportunity just then of telling him what she wanted to disclose about her sister .

sections.label: Section 4.1.621
sections.original: Invitat tristis ; mansit : ibi illa cum illo sermonem occipit .
sections.translation: He was invited in , and took his seat . Then she entered into discourse with him . But the Captain , fancying it was a rival brought before his very eyes , wanted in his turn to mortify her : Hark you , boy , said he , go fetch Pamphila , that she may amuse us here . She exclaimed , At a banquet ! Certainly not . The Captain still persisted to a downright quarrel .

sections.label: Section 4.1.626
sections.original: Interea aurum sibi clam mulier demit ; dat mihi ut auferam .
sections.translation: Meanwhile my mistress secretly took off her golden jewels , [*] and gave them to me to take away : this is a sign , I ’ m sure , that she ’ ll betake herself from there as soon as she possibly can .

sections.label: Section 4.2.628
sections.original: Dum rus eo coepi egomet mecum inter vias ,
sections.translation: While I was going [*] into the country , I began on the road , as it mostly happens when there is any anxiety on the mind ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.630
sections.original: Aliam rem ex alia cogitare , et ea omnia in
sections.translation: to reflect with myself upon one thing after another , and upon every thing in the worst light . What need of words ? While I was musing thus , inadvertently I passed my country-house . I had already got some distance from it , when I perceived this ; I returned again , really feeling quite uneasy ; when I came to the very turning that leads to the house , I came to a stop ,

sections.label: Section 4.2.635
sections.original: Occepi mecum cogitare : Hem ! biduum hic
sections.translation: and began to reason with myself ; What ! must I stay here alone for two days without her ? Well , and what then ? It ’ s nothing at all . What ? Nothing at all ? Well now , if I haven ' t the privilege of touching her , am I not even to have that of seeing her ? If I may not do the one , at least I may the other . Surely to love at a distance [*]

sections.label: Section 4.2.640
sections.original: Amare haud nihil est . Villam praetereo sciens .
sections.translation: even , is better than nothing at all . I purposely passed the house . But how ’ s this , that Pythias is suddenly hurrying out in such a fright ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.642
sections.original: Ubi ego illum scelerosum misera atque impium inveniam ? aut ubi quaeram ?
sections.translation: Where , wretch that I am , shall I find this wicked and impious fellow ? Or where look for him ? That he should dare to commit so audacious a crime as this ! I ’ m ruined outright !

sections.label: Section 4.3.643b
sections.original: Perii ! hoc quid sit vereor .
sections.translation: I dread what this may be .

sections.label: Section 4.3.644
sections.original: Quin etiam insuper scelus , postquam ludificatus est virginem ,
sections.translation: Besides , too , the villain , after he had abused the girl ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.645
sections.original: Vestem omnem miserae discidit ; tum ipsam capillo conscidit .
sections.translation: rent all the poor thing ’ s clothes , and tore her hair as well .

sections.label: Section 4.3.646
sections.original: Hem .
sections.translation: Ha !

sections.label: Section 4.3.646b
sections.original: Qui nunc si detur mihi ,
sections.translation: If he were just now in my reach , how eagerly would I fly at that villain ’ s eyes with my nails !

sections.label: Section 4.3.648
sections.original: Nescio , quid profecto absente nobis turbatum est domi .
sections.translation: Really I can ’ t imagine what disturbance has happened to us at home in my absence . I ’ ll accost them . ( Going up to them .) What ’ s the matter ? Why in such haste ? Or whom are you looking for , Pythias ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.650
sections.original: Ehem , Phaedria , ego quem quaeram ? ine hinc quo dignus es cum donis tuis
sections.translation: Why , Phaedria , whom should I be looking for ? Away with you , as you deserve , with such fine presents of yours .

sections.label: Section 4.3.651b
sections.original: Quid istuc est rei ?
sections.translation: What is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.652
sections.original: Rogas me ? Eunuchum quem dedisti nobis quas turbas dedit !
sections.translation: What , do you ask ? The Eunuch you gave us , what confusion he has caused . He has ravished the girl whom the Captain made present of to my mistress .

sections.label: Section 4.3.653b
sections.original: Quid ais ?
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.654
sections.original: Perii .
sections.translation: I ’ m ruined outright !

sections.label: Section 4.3.654b
sections.original: Temulenta es .
sections.translation: You are drunk .

sections.label: Section 4.3.654c
sections.original: Utinam sic sient qui mihi male volunt .
sections.translation: I wish that they were so , who wish ill to me .

sections.label: Section 4.3.655
sections.original: Au , obsecro , mea Pythias , quid istuc nam monstri fuit ?
sections.translation: Oh , prithee , my dear Pythias , what a monstrous thing this is !

sections.label: Section 4.3.656
sections.original: Insanis : qui istuc facere eunuchus potuit ?
sections.translation: You are out of your senses . How could a Eunuch possibly do this ?

sections.label: Section 4.3.656b
sections.original: Ego illum nescio
sections.translation: I know nothing about him : as to what he has done , the thing speaks for itself . The girl is in tears ; and when you ask her what ’ s the matter , she does not dare tell . But he , a precious fellow , is nowhere to be seen . To my sorrow I suspect too ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.660
sections.original: Aliquid domo abeuntem abstulisse .
sections.translation: that when he took himself off he carried something away from the house .

sections.label: Section 4.3.660b
sections.original: Nequeo mirari satis
sections.translation: I can not enough wonder , whither this varlet can possibly have betaken himself to any distance from here ; unless perhaps he has returned home to our house .

sections.label: Section 4.3.662b
sections.original: Vise amabo num sit .
sections.translation: Pray , go and see whether he is there .

sections.label: Section 4.3.662c
sections.original: Iam faxo scies .
sections.translation: I ’ ll let you know immediately .

sections.label: Section 4.3.663
sections.original: Perii ! obsecro tam infandum facinus , mea tu , ne audivi quidem .
sections.translation: Ruined outright ! Prithee , my dear , I never did so much as hear of a deed so abominable !

sections.label: Section 4.3.664
sections.original: At pol ego amatores mulierum esse audieram eos maximos ,
sections.translation: Why , faith , I had heard that they were extremely fond of the women ,

sections.label: Section 4.3.665
sections.original: Sed nihil potesse ; verum miserae non in mentem venerat ;
sections.translation: but were incapable ; unfortunately what has happened never came into my mind ; otherwise I should have shut him up somewhere , and not have intrusted the girl to him .

sections.label: Section 4.4.667
sections.original: Exi foras , sceleste : at etiam restitas
sections.translation: Come out , you villain ! What , do you lag behind , you runaway ? Out with you , you sorry bargain !
